title: Store Intelligence — Sales
description: Retail sales performance analytics: revenue, orders, margins, product/category/store trends, and period comparisons.

question: How did the Northeast region perform last week?
What are the top 10 selling products this month?
Compare this week vs last week revenue by region
Which departments have the highest margin?

description: Pre-joined order + line-item data with store, product, category, and region dimensions. One row per order line. Prefer this view for most queries.

content: You are a Retail Sales Analyst. Help store managers and executives understand sales performance.
Always use the retail schema prefix.
Default time range: if the user does not specify, use the last 7 days.
Sales are at store level. The orders table has both str_id and reg_id. Report at store or region level.
Prefer vw_sales_summary for most queries — it pre-joins orders, line items, products, categories, stores, and regions.
Revenue = ln_total_amt (line total) or ord_total_amt (order total). Use line-level for product breakdowns.
Margin = line_margin_amt, calculated as (unit_price_amt - unit_cost_amt) * qty.
Do not expose customer IDs. Only use for aggregate counts.
When asked how a store or region is doing, provide: total revenue, order count, units sold, avg basket size, top categories.
Format currency as USD with 2 decimal places. Format large numbers with commas.

question: How did the Northeast region perform last week?
sql:
SELECT
    reg_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ord_id) AS total_orders,
    SUM(qty) AS total_units,
    SUM(line_total) AS total_revenue,
    SUM(line_margin_amt) AS total_margin,
    SUM(line_total) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT ord_id), 0) AS avg_basket_size
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE reg_name = 'Northeast'
  AND order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 7)
GROUP BY reg_name

question: What are the top 10 selling products this month?
sql:
SELECT
    prod_name, brand_name, cat_name,
    SUM(qty) AS total_units,
    SUM(line_total) AS total_revenue
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE())
GROUP BY prod_name, brand_name, cat_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show me daily revenue trend for the Southeast region
sql:
SELECT
    order_date,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ord_id) AS orders,
    SUM(line_total) AS revenue
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE reg_name = 'Southeast'
  AND order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 30)
GROUP BY order_date
ORDER BY order_date

question: Which departments have the highest margin?
sql:
SELECT
    depa_name,
    SUM(line_total) AS revenue,
    SUM(line_margin_amt) AS margin,
    SUM(line_margin_amt) / NULLIF(SUM(line_total), 0) * 100 AS margin_pct
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 30)
GROUP BY depa_name
ORDER BY margin DESC

question: How many expedited orders did we have this week?
sql:
SELECT
    reg_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ord_id) AS expedited_orders,
    SUM(line_total) AS expedited_revenue
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE is_expedited = 1
  AND order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 7)
GROUP BY reg_name
ORDER BY expedited_orders DESC

question: Compare this week vs last week revenue by region
sql:
SELECT
    reg_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 7)
             THEN line_total ELSE 0 END) AS this_week_revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 14)
              AND order_date < DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 7)
             THEN line_total ELSE 0 END) AS last_week_revenue
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 14)
GROUP BY reg_name
ORDER BY reg_name

question: Payment method breakdown
sql:
SELECT
    paym_method_type,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ord_id) AS order_count,
    SUM(line_total) AS revenue
FROM ${catalog}.retail.vw_sales_summary
WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 30)
GROUP BY paym_method_type
ORDER BY revenue DESC

comment: Join orders to order_lines on ord_id
instruction: Use this join for line-level sales analysis

comment: Join order_lines to products on prod_id
instruction: Use to get product details for line items

comment: Join orders to stores on str_id
instruction: Use to get store details for orders

comment: Join orders to regions on reg_id
instruction: Use to get region details for orders

comment: Join products to product_categories on prod_cat_id
instruction: Use to get category and department for products
Default time filter when user does not specify a date range
